skill.name: frontend-slides
skill.description: Create animation-rich HTML slide decks from new content, rough notes, or PowerPoint files, with guided style discovery and browser-based delivery.
skill.summary: Build polished HTML presentations and convert PowerPoint decks into animated web slides.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, crypto, key, and obfuscation patterns. Review found most are Markdown fences, prose, CSS examples, and UI text. Real risks are workspace file writes, external font loading, cleanup instructions, and a browser open command.

medium_findings.title: Local File Creation and Cleanup Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill creates preview HTML files, asset directories, extracted images, and temporary files. This is expected, but it can modify the workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit in the skill instructions. They are scoped to presentation previews, output assets, and user-provided PowerPoint content.

medium_findings.title: External Font Stylesheet Loading in Generated HTML
medium_findings.description: The generated HTML template can load Fontshare or Google Fonts. This contacts third-party font services when the deck opens.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded font URL is visible in the HTML template. No reviewed line sends secrets or document content.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Many Ruby backtick findings match Markdown fences, inline file paths, and examples. They are not executable Ruby shell calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines show Markdown fences and prose examples. A separate browser open instruction is tracked as a real command risk.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto and Key Findings Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak crypto and key alerts match metadata, keyboard text, style labels, and related skill names. No cryptographic code or secrets were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations contain metadata, CSS, UI text, and related skill names. The scanner appears to match generic substrings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace File Writes from PPT Extraction
dangerous_patterns.description: The PPT extraction example writes image blobs into an assets directory. This should stay inside the active project workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code example creates a directory and writes image bytes. The displayed operation uses output_dir and generated asset names.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Launch Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The delivery instructions tell the agent to run an operating-system open command. This launches the generated HTML file locally.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command instruction is explicit and tied to the produced file. The target is described as the generated presentation filename.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Font Request
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated HTML may include an external font stylesheet URL. Offline or private decks should use local or system fonts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external URL is directly shown in the template. No reviewed line sends credentials or document content.

content.value_statement: Designing a polished presentation from notes or PowerPoint can take significant time. This skill guides content structure, visual style selection, and HTML slide generation.
actual_capabilities: Creates self-contained HTML presentations with inline CSS and JavaScript.
Generates three visual style previews before building the full deck.
Converts PowerPoint content and images into web presentation assets.
Adds keyboard, scroll, touch, progress, and navigation dot controls.
Includes accessibility and responsive design guidance for generated slides.
Provides animation patterns for cinematic, technical, professional, and editorial styles.
limitations: PowerPoint conversion depends on Python and the python-pptx package.
Generated decks may load external fonts unless replaced with local fonts.
Complex PowerPoint layouts may need manual review after extraction.
The skill gives instructions and examples; final quality depends on user content.

use_cases.title: Create an investor pitch deck
use_cases.description: Turn startup notes into a styled HTML pitch deck with motion and presentation controls.

use_cases.title: Convert training slides to web
use_cases.description: Extract text and images from a PowerPoint file and rebuild them as browser-based slides.

use_cases.title: Polish a conference talk
use_cases.description: Explore visual directions and generate a responsive deck for a technical or product talk.

prompt_templates.title: Start a new deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete presentation from a topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 10-slide HTML presentation about our product launch. Ask me for the missing content and style preferences first.

prompt_templates.title: Use rough notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Organize existing bullets into slides before design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these rough notes into a concise web slide deck. Propose the slide structure, then create style previews.

prompt_templates.title: Convert PowerPoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Rebuild an existing deck as a browser presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PowerPoint file into an animated HTML presentation. Preserve slide order, text, images, and speaker notes where possible.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an existing HTML deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for visual and interaction improvements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this HTML presentation and improve the visual hierarchy, transitions, responsiveness, and accessibility without changing the main message.

output_examples.input: A founder provides bullet points for an AI startup pitch.
output_examples.output: A structured pitch deck with three style previews, a selected visual direction, and a finished HTML presentation file.

output_examples.input: A trainer uploads a PowerPoint workshop deck.
output_examples.output: Extracted slide content, saved image assets, a confirmation summary, and a rebuilt browser-based training presentation.

output_examples.input: A speaker asks to improve an existing web deck.
output_examples.output: A refined presentation with stronger typography, smoother navigation, responsive layouts, and reduced-motion support.

best_practices: Share the audience, goal, slide count, and source content before asking for design work.
Review the three style previews before generating the full deck.
Use local fonts and assets when the presentation must work offline or stay private.
anti_patterns: Do not skip content discovery when the message and slide order are unclear.
Do not rely on external font links for confidential or offline presentations.
Do not assume PowerPoint extraction preserves every layout detail automatically.

faq.question: Can it create a deck from only a topic?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for context, helps structure the content, and then generates visual style previews.

faq.question: Can it convert PPTX files?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python extraction workflow for slide text, images, and notes.

faq.question: Does the output need a build step?
faq.answer: No. The intended output is a self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JavaScript.

faq.question: Can the deck work offline?
faq.answer: Yes, if external fonts and remote assets are replaced with local or system resources.

faq.question: Does it support mobile viewing?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions include responsive layouts, touch navigation, and reduced heavy effects on smaller screens.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive presentations?
faq.answer: Use local fonts and keep generated files in a controlled workspace. The audit found no credential exfiltration evidence.
